mnemonics.title: C++ Program Structure
mnemonics.content:
I = Include (#include)
C = Comments
M = main() function
A = Add statements
I = Include return
N = aNd close brace
- Think: ICMAIN - The main components to remember

acronyms.usage: The name indicates it extends C with OOP features
Standard input stream for reading data
Standard output stream for displaying data
Header file for I/O operations in C++

visualTips.visualization: Draw a top-down flowchart: Preprocessor → main() start → Statements → return → main() end. This shows the linear execution path.
Remember: cin>> flows INTO variables (extraction), cout<< flows OUT to screen (insertion). Arrow direction shows data flow direction.
Think of braces as containers that hold the function body together. Everything inside belongs to that function.
Imagine std as a labeled box containing standard items. Using std:: is like reaching into that labeled box to get cout.

analogies.analogy: main() is like the main gate of a building. Everyone must enter and exit through this gate - it's the only official entrance/exit.
#include is like requesting documents from an office before a meeting. The documents (header files) are gathered before the actual meeting (compilation) begins.
Comments are like sticky notes on a document. They help humans understand but are ignored by the actual process (compiler doesn't read them).
Think of cout as a conveyor belt going OUT to the screen. The << operator pushes (inserts) data onto this outward-bound belt.
cin is like a vacuum cleaner pulling (extracting) data from the keyboard into the variable. The >> operator pulls data IN.
